name: smithed

metadata.name: smithed
metadata.summary: Smithed's Python client with CLI, weld and more

documentation:
# smithed-python
The python package for all things Smithed. Includes weld, cli, and libraries (via [`smithed-libraries`](https://github.com/Smithed-MC/Libraries)).

## Weld
> The fastest pack merger of the west 🚅, build on beet technologies™

- [Docs](https://wiki.smithed.dev/weld/)

```bash
pip install smithed
```

## Contributing

We use [rye](https://rye-up.com/) to manage this project's dependencies. This will handle every aspect from grabbing the correct python, downloading dependencies, and managing a virtual environment.

To get started, install rye:
- [smithed-python](#smithed-python)
  - [Weld](#weld)
  - [Contributing](#contributing)
    - [MacOS and Linux](#macos-and-linux)
    - [Windows](#windows)
  - [Developer Setup](#developer-setup)

### MacOS and Linux

```bash
curl -sSf https://rye-up.com/get | bash
```

### Windows

Check the guide [here](https://rye-up.com/guide/installation/#installing-rye) for an in-depth explanation.

## Developer Setup

Once installed, you can sync the project to generate a virtual environment and you'll be set!

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Smithed-MC/smithed-python
cd smithed-python
rye sync
```

functions.name: smithed.utils.path_str
documentation: Returns str of a path, replacing the home directory with '~'

functions.name: smithed.utils.format_error
documentation: Format a given error message and exception.

functions.name: smithed.utils.error_handler
documentation: Context manager that catches and displays exceptions.

methods.name: smithed.weld.models.main.SmithedModel.push_down_priorities
documentation:
Push down top-level priority to every rule.

If a rule has a priority defined, it will not be overwritten.

class_variables.name: smithed.weld.models.main.SmithedModel.override
documentation: only should be set by bundle packs

classes.name: smithed.weld.models.main.SmithedJsonFile

methods.name: smithed.weld.models.main.SmithedJsonFile.convert_rules
documentation:
Converts the source field of additive rules to a value source if it is
a reference source, essentially "baking" it in for ease of use later.

Accepts any standard JSON file from in-game, only needs as `__smithed__` field
for custom merging logic

class_variables.name: smithed.weld.models.base.BaseModel.Config.json_encoders
documentation: type: ignore

functions.name: smithed.weld.webapp.merging.weld_packs
documentation: Welds a list of zip files. Outputs a path

documentation: Error resulting from the merging process

classes.name: smithed.weld.merging.errors.PriorityError
documentation: Error related to resolving priorities

classes.name: smithed.weld.merging.errors.ParsingError
documentation: Error related to parsing json path

modules.name: smithed.weld.merging
documentation: Contains logic for merging json files via beet merge policies

documentation: Prints the merging pack name

documentation:

preprocessing later in the process.

params.name: typer.Argument(help="A series of packs to weld")

documentation:
Weld data and resource packs together with all data and assets in one zip.

See the [docs](https://wiki.smithed.dev/weld) more info!

Push down top-level priority to every rule.

If a rule has a priority defined, it will not be overwritten.

modules.name: smithed.weld.merging.parser
documentation: This file is super scrappy and needs to be rewritten but I can't be bothered right now.

modules.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler
documentation:
This is the main logic for weld's custom merging logic.

It uses the beet's merge policies to implement a conflict handler that registers
 conflicts, merging all `__smithed__` into a base file (whether it's vanilla or not).
 In a later invocation, it then processes all of the registered conflicts, determines
 the ordering via the priority and stage system, and applies each defined rule onto
 the base file.

 TODO: Likely refactor into multiple files, I can see each file handling being it's own
  class since several methods based on each file are passing similar parameters with
  each other.

functions.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.dedupe_conflict
documentation:
This dedupe goes through all of the rules in the conflict file, ditches them if
it already exists in the currently loaded rules. It looks pretty unperformant but
these lists shouldn't be too large so it's alright. We need to keep the order and
using sets would require me to make everything hashable.

functions.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.normalize_quotes
documentation: It's a bit odd but we need some normalization

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.__call__
documentation: Register conflicts..

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.parse_smithed_file
documentation: Parses a smithed file and returns the parsed file or False if invalid.

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.grab_vanilla
documentation:
Grabs the vanilla file to load as the current file (aka the base).

⚠️ Uses the bundled `yellow_shulker_box.json` as an override over vanilla's.

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.process
documentation: Main entrypoint for smithed merge solving

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.process_file
documentation: Process each file's rules

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.manage_indexes
documentation: Adds / removes `_index` field to every item in a list

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.pre_process_condition
documentation: Returns true if all conditions satisfy their criteria.

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.pre_process_rules
documentation:
Gathers models by id. Pre-processes models for ease of use.

Converts each 'before' priority into 'after' (as it's easier to resolve).

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.resolve_priorities
documentation: Resolves priorities for each stage.

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.resolve_stage
documentation:
Resolves priorities within a specific stage.

⚠️ Raises PriorityError if a dependency loop is found.
⚠️ Raises PriorityError if a pack depends on another pack from a different stage.
⚠️ Logs warning if a pack depends on a pack that does not exist.

methods.name: smithed.weld.merging.handler.ConflictsHandler.apply_rule
documentation:
Rule application uses `jsonpath_ng` for parsing target paths.

TODO: extract outward, maybe bundle rule application with the rule itself
